class.name: PopupMenu
class.description: A class that implements a menu which can be dynamically popped up at a specified position within a component. As the inheritance hierarchy implies, a PopupMenu can be used anywhere a Menu can be used. However, if you use a PopupMenu like a Menu (e.g., you add it to a MenuBar), then you cannot call show on that PopupMenu.

constructors.name: PopupMenu
constructors.description: Creates a new popup menu with an empty name.

constructors.name: PopupMenu
constructors.description: Creates a new popup menu with the specified name.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: a non-null string specifying the popup menu's label

methods.name: addNotify
methods.description: Creates the popup menu's peer. The peer allows us to change the appearance of the popup menu without changing any of the popup menu's functionality.

methods.name: show
methods.signature: public void show(Component origin, int x, int y)
methods.description: Shows the popup menu at the x, y position relative to an origin component. The origin component must be contained within the component hierarchy of the popup menu's parent. Both the origin and the parent must be showing on the screen for this method to be valid. If this PopupMenu is being used as a Menu (i.e., it has a non-Component parent), then you cannot call this method on the PopupMenu.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: the component which defines the coordinate space

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: the x coordinate position to popup the menu

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: the y coordinate position to popup the menu

throws.description: if this PopupMenu has a non-Component parent
if the origin is not in the parent's hierarchy
if the parent is not showing on screen

methods.name: getAccessibleContext
methods.description: Gets the AccessibleContext associated with this PopupMenu.
